title: Getting back to your hotel after 10 PM
context: You're leaving a bar in West Hollywood or a show on the Sunset Strip.
advice: Skip the late-night walk — open Uber or Lyft right from the venue. LA blocks empty out fast at night and distances are deceiving. Wait inside until your driver is a minute away.

title: Parking your rental car safely
context: You've driven to a trailhead, beach lot, or street spot in an unfamiliar area.
advice: Never leave bags, electronics, or anything visible in the car — smash-and-grabs are the most common LA tourist crime. Use paid or attended lots where you can, and photograph your spot.

title: Feeling isolated without a group
context: It's day two and the car-dependent sprawl has you feeling cut off from people.
advice: Pick social-by-design activities: a free DTLA walking tour, a surf lesson in Santa Monica, a Meetup hike, or bar seating at a busy restaurant. LA rewards travelers who seek connection out.

title: Using the Metro solo
context: You're taking the E Line from Santa Monica to Downtown during off-peak hours.
advice: Sit near the front car and the operator, keep your phone away on the platform, and avoid near-empty stations late at night. For anything after the evening, choose rideshare instead.
